message:
fix(codec): compute PDU data_len from observed leb128 bytes, not canonical re-encoding

decode_raw and decode_raw_async previously derived the data_len by calling
encoded_length(ident) + encoded_length(serial) and subtracting from the
framed length. encoded_length re-encodes each value in its canonical shortest
form, so any sender that emitted a valid-but-non-canonical leb128 header
(e.g. a 2-byte encoding for a value that fits in 1 byte) would fail decode
with "sizes don't make sense" even though the frame is well-formed per the
leb128 spec.

## The fix: observe, don't reconstruct

Add length-returning variants of the leb128 readers that expose how many
bytes were actually consumed:

  read_u64_async_with_len<R>(r) -> anyhow::Result<(u64, usize)>
  read_u64_with_len<R>(r)       -> anyhow::Result<(u64, usize)>

Both are thin wrappers around the existing byte-at-a-time feed loop; the
only new behavior is returning buf.len() alongside the decoded u64. The
existing read_u64_async / read_u64 functions now delegate to the _with_len
variants and discard the length, preserving all current callers.

decode_raw and decode_raw_async switch to the _with_len readers, sum the
observed header bytes with a checked_add (with an anyhow::Context for the
overflow case — not physically reachable on wire data but types force it),
and compute data_len from the observed header footprint instead of the
canonical re-encoding. "sizes don't make sense" still triggers when the
arithmetic underflows (the real detection surface) — the error message is
updated to surface serial_len / ident_len instead of
encoded_length(serial) / encoded_length(ident), which is strictly more
informative: it now shows what the sender ACTUALLY wrote, not what a
canonical encoder WOULD have written.

## Regression coverage

Two new tests in frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs (the sync + async decoders
must agree on the same wire fixture):

  - decode_accepts_valid_non_canonical_leb128_headers:
    wire = [0x84, 0x00, 0x81, 0x00, 0xE3, 0x00]
    (payload_len=4 over 2 bytes, serial=1 over 2 bytes, ident=99 over 2 bytes,
    zero-byte body). Asserts serial=1 and pdu = Pdu::Invalid { ident: 99 }.

  - decode_raw_async_accepts_valid_non_canonical_leb128_headers: same
    fixture through Pdu::decode_async over an async Cursor, asserting the
    same parsed values.

## Proptest harness refactor (same change, surfaces second bug)

frankenterm/codec/tests/proptest_codec_decode_fuzz.rs extracts the frame
assembly code from the random_header proptest into a shared helper
`structured_header_frame(ident, serial, body, is_compressed)`. While
extracting, the compression-flag bit was wrong: the proptest flipped
1u64 << 62 but the real codec's COMPRESSED_MASK is 1u64 << 63. That meant
the compressed branch of the fuzz harness was testing against a bit the
decoder doesn't check — so the harness silently never exercised the
compressed path for the last ~2048 cases per run.

Fix by pulling COMPRESSED_MASK = 1u64 << 63 into the test file as a const
(mirroring the lib.rs constant) and using it inside the helper. Add a
targeted unit test `structured_header_helper_uses_real_codec_compression_bit`
that roundtrips a PING_IDENT=1 Ping frame through the helper — uncompressed
decodes cleanly, compressed-flagged empty body hits the zstd decode path
and returns a typed error (not a panic). This pins the helper against the
real codec's constant so future drift between the two is caught immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
